---
title: "About"
subtitle: "Data dictionary, provenance, and citations"
---

## About this site

`samcaldwell.info` is a static, open-source interactive analysis of the US
economy in 2026 placed against the prior 25 years. Every chart is an
interactive JavaScript widget rendered into a self-contained HTML page.

**This prototype ships with a calibrated synthetic dataset** — historical
values are hand-entered references based on public releases, and values for
years ≥ 2025 are *illustrative* (not forecasts). No proprietary data is
redistributed. See citations below.

---

## Data dictionary

### `annual.csv` — 2001–2026, one row per calendar year

| Column | Unit | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `year` | integer | Calendar year (2001–2026) |
| `gdp_growth` | % | Real GDP growth, year-over-year (proxy for BEA series GDPC1) |
| `unemployment` | % | Civilian unemployment rate, annual average (BLS LNS14000000) |
| `cpi` | % | CPI-U inflation, year-over-year (BLS CUUR0000SA0) |
| `fed_funds` | % | Federal Funds target rate, end-of-period (Federal Reserve) |
| `ten_year` | % | 10-year Treasury constant-maturity yield, annual average (US Treasury) |
| `sp500_ret` | % | S&P 500 annual total return |
| `dow_ret` | % | Dow Jones Industrial Average annual price return |
| `nasdaq_ret` | % | NASDAQ Composite annual price return |
| `vix_avg` | index level | CBOE Volatility Index (VIX), annual mean of daily close |
| `recession` | 0 / 1 | NBER recession indicator — 1 if any part of the year was a recession |
| `prototype` | 0 / 1 | 1 = synthetic/illustrative value (years ≥ 2025); 0 = calibrated from real public releases |

### `quarterly.csv` — 2001Q1 – 2026Q1

| Column | Unit | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `year`, `quarter` | integer | Year and quarter (1–4) |
| `date` | ISO date | First day of the quarter |
| `period` | label | `YYYYQn` (e.g., `2023Q4`) |
| `gdp_growth` | % | Quarterly real GDP growth (YoY proxy) |
| `unemployment` | % | Quarterly average unemployment rate |
| `cpi` | % | Quarterly CPI YoY |
| `fed_funds` | % | Quarterly average Federal Funds rate |
| `ten_year` | % | Quarterly average 10Y Treasury yield |
| `sp500_ret_qoq` | % | S&P 500 quarter-over-quarter return |
| `vix` | index level | Quarterly average VIX |

### `monthly.csv` — 2001-01 through most recent month

| Column | Unit | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `year`, `month` | integer | Year and month (1–12) |
| `date` | ISO date | First day of the month |
| `unemployment` | % | Monthly unemployment rate |
| `cpi` | % | Monthly CPI YoY |
| `fed_funds` | % | Monthly Federal Funds rate |
| `ten_year` | % | Monthly 10Y Treasury yield |
| `vix` | index level | Monthly average VIX |
| `sp500_level` | index level | S&P 500 month-end level |

### `gdp_components.csv` — expenditure shares

| Column | Unit | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `year` | integer | Calendar year |
| `consumption` | % of GDP | Personal Consumption Expenditures (C) |
| `investment` | % of GDP | Gross Private Domestic Investment (I) |
| `government` | % of GDP | Government Consumption + Investment (G) |
| `net_exports` | % of GDP | Net Exports of Goods & Services (NX) |

Shares sum to ~100 by construction.

### `sectors.csv` — S&P 500 GICS sector returns

| Column | Unit | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `year` | integer | Calendar year |
| `sector` | text | GICS Level-1 sector (e.g., Information Technology, Financials) |
| `return_pct` | % | Annual total return for the sector |

---

## Data provenance

**Years 2001–2024** — values are hand-entered from public references at the
time of prototyping. Because official series are revised periodically, these
may differ slightly from current vintage-adjusted values.

**Years 2025 and onward** — values are *illustrative prototype values*. They
are marked `prototype = 1` in the annual CSV and visually flagged with an
asterisk (`*`) throughout the site.

**Quarterly and monthly series** — decomposed from the annual values with
seeded pseudo-random noise such that the within-year averages match the
annual figure. High-frequency movements are therefore illustrative, not real
historical paths.

**Sector returns** — derived from synthetic S&P 500 returns via published
historical sector betas plus noise. Not a reconstruction of actual sector
performance.

---

## Citations

### Public-domain US government data

US government works are in the public domain under 17 U.S.C. § 105 and may
be freely reused.

1. **U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis.** *Real Gross Domestic Product* [GDPC1].
   Retrieved from Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis, FRED.
   <https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/GDPC1>
2. **U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis.** *National Income and Product Accounts
   Tables — Gross Domestic Product: Components*.
   <https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product>
3. **U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.** *Civilian Unemployment Rate* [UNRATE /
   LNS14000000]. <https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/UNRATE>
4. **U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.** *Consumer Price Index for All Urban
   Consumers: All Items in U.S. City Average* [CPIAUCSL / CUUR0000SA0].
   <https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL>
5. **Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System (US).** *Federal Funds
   Effective Rate* [FEDFUNDS]. <https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS>
6. **Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System (US).** *Market Yield on
   U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an
   Investment Basis* [DGS10]. <https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10>
7. **National Bureau of Economic Research.** *US Business Cycle Expansions and
   Contractions* (recession dating).
   <https://www.nber.org/research/data/us-business-cycle-expansions-and-contractions>

### Proprietary third-party data — attribution only, not redistributed

The series below are referenced for analytical context. Their index names are
trademarks of the respective owners and are used nominatively; no affiliation
or endorsement is implied. This site **does not redistribute the underlying
proprietary series** — historical values are synthesized from public
commentary and the site's current dataset is the calibrated prototype.

8. **S&P Dow Jones Indices LLC.** *S&P 500 Index®*. "S&P®" and "S&P 500®" are
   registered trademarks of Standard & Poor's Financial Services LLC, a
   division of S&P Global. <https://www.spglobal.com/spdji/>
9. **S&P Dow Jones Indices LLC / Dow Jones & Company, Inc.** *Dow Jones
   Industrial Average®*. "Dow Jones®" and "DJIA®" are trademarks of
   S&P Dow Jones Indices LLC and/or Dow Jones & Company, Inc.
10. **Nasdaq, Inc.** *NASDAQ Composite Index®*. "NASDAQ®" is a registered
    trademark of Nasdaq, Inc. <https://www.nasdaq.com/>
11. **Cboe Global Markets, Inc.** *Cboe Volatility Index® (VIX®)*. "VIX®" and
    "Cboe®" are registered trademarks of Cboe Global Markets, Inc.
    <https://www.cboe.com/tradable_products/vix/>
12. **Global Industry Classification Standard (GICS®)** — sector taxonomy is
    jointly owned by MSCI Inc. and S&P Global. Sector names used here follow
    GICS Level 1. <https://www.msci.com/our-solutions/indexes/gics>

### FRED terms of use

Where FRED is cited as the retrieval endpoint, the FRED Terms of Use apply:
public-domain series are unrestricted; proprietary licensed series may not
be redistributed without the data owner's permission. See
<https://fred.stlouisfed.org/legal/#copyright>.

---

## Open-source stack

| Component | Role | License |
|---|---|---|
| [Quarto](https://quarto.org) | Authoring + site generator | MIT |
| [Apache ECharts](https://echarts.apache.org/) via [echarts4r](https://echarts4r.john-coene.com/) | Drill-down charts (sunburst, treemap, bars, lines, heatmap) | Apache 2.0 |
| [Plotly.js](https://plotly.com/javascript/) via [plotly (R)](https://plotly-r.com/) | Scatter / linked charts | MIT |
| [reactable](https://glin.github.io/reactable/) | Interactive tables | MIT |
| [jQuery Sparkline](https://omnipotent.net/jquery.sparkline/) via [sparkline (R)](https://github.com/htmlwidgets/sparkline) | Inline mini-charts | BSD-3 |
| [crosstalk](https://rstudio.github.io/crosstalk/) | Client-side linked filtering | MIT |
| [Bootstrap](https://getbootstrap.com/) (via Quarto's `cosmo` theme) | Page layout & typography | MIT |

## Code license

The source code for this site is released under the MIT License. See the
[`LICENSE`](https://github.com/sam-caldwell/samcaldwell.info/blob/main/LICENSE)
file in the repository for the full text.

---

## Disclaimer

Nothing on this site constitutes investment, tax, legal, or financial advice.
The dataset is a calibrated synthetic prototype provided for illustration of
the visualization stack; it should not be used as a source of record for any
economic, trading, or reporting decision.
